events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "critical",
  "work_stage": "in-progress",
  "component_conditions": ["non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 30,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Stored energy source not isolated — hydraulic, gravity, or capacitor energy remains uncontrolled during active work",
    "Lockout/Tagout procedure incomplete: energy isolation verified for primary source but stored/residual energy not released or blocked",
    "No zero-energy verification performed for all energy types present",
    "Workers exposed to unexpected energization or stored energy release during in-progress work"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop work immediately and clear all personnel from the hazard zone",
    "Identify all energy sources including stored/residual energy: hydraulic accumulators, gravity-loaded components, capacitor banks, springs, pneumatic reservoirs",
    "Release or safely dissipate all stored energy — bleed hydraulic lines, block or lower gravity-loaded equipment, discharge capacitors, release spring tension",
    "Apply lockout/tagout devices to all energy isolation points and verify zero-energy state before resuming work",
    "Test-start controls to confirm equipment cannot energize after LOTO is applied",
    "Retrain workers on complete stored energy isolation per OSHA 1910.147 before resuming"
  ],
  "rationale": "OSHA 1910.147 requires isolation of all energy sources including stored energy (hydraulic, gravity, capacitor, spring, pneumatic). Missing stored energy isolation during active work creates a critical risk of unexpected energization or sudden release. Work must stop immediately until zero-energy state is verified.",
  "references": ["OSHA 1910.147(c)(1)", "OSHA 1910.147(d)(4)", "OSHA 1910.147(d)(5)", "OSHA 1910.147(d)(6)"]
}
